lede: A look at November 18 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Kim Wilde and Allyson Felix, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Texas A&M University
events.desc: At Texas A&M University, the Aggie Bonfire collapses killing 12 students and injuring 27 others.

events.title: South Africa
events.desc: In South Africa, 21 political parties approve a new constitution, expanding voting rights and ending white minority rule.

events.title: King's Cross fire
events.desc: King's Cross fire: In London, 31 people die in a fire at the city's busiest underground station, King's Cross St Pancras.

events.title: Oman
events.desc: Oman declares its independence from the United Kingdom.

events.title: Vietnam War
events.desc: Vietnam War: United States President John F. Kennedy sends 18,000 military advisors to South Vietnam.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: German leader Adolf Hitler and Italian Foreign Minister Galeazzo Ciano meet to discuss Benito Mussolini's disastrous Italian invasion of Greece.

events.title: Latvia
events.desc: Latvia declares its independence from Russia.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: First Battle of the Somme: In France, British Expeditionary Force commander Douglas Haig calls off the battle which started on July 1, 1916.

events.title: Napoleonic Wars
events.desc: Napoleonic Wars: In a naval action, French frigates defeat British East Indiamen in the Bay of Bengal.

events.title: Christopher Columbus
events.desc: Christopher Columbus first sights the island now known as Puerto Rico.

births.name: Mahinda Rajapaksa
births.line: Sri Lankan lawyer and politician, 6th President of Sri Lanka

deaths.text: Jim Jones, American cult leader, founded Peoples Temple
Niels Bohr, Danish footballer, physicist, and academic, Nobel Prize laureate
Chris Watson, Chilean-Australian journalist and politician, 3rd Prime Minister of Australia
Chester A. Arthur, American general, lawyer, and politician, 21st President of the United States

observances.text: Christian feast day: Abhai of Hach (Syriac Orthodox Church)
Christian feast day: Dedication of Saints Peter and Paul
Christian feast day: Elizabeth of Hungary (Church of England)
Christian feast day: Mabyn (Roman Catholic Church and Anglicanism)
